leads.name: Open Letter Books
leads.title: Publisher
leads.subj: Printing your translation list -- short runs from Ann Arbor
leads.opener: This is an introduction, nothing more. Open Letter is a literary press working in translation, and presses like yours are the work we do most. We would like to be on the list of printers you quote when your next season is ready. That is the entire ask.
leads.b3: Backlist reprints in small quantities, so your library collections stay in stock without filling a warehouse.
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary translation press housed at the University of Rochester, publishing about 10 titles a year per their own about page. They sell backlist collections direct to libraries, which means recurring small reprint runs rather than one large annual print buy.
leads.pitch: Translation press with a library-direct backlist model that generates steady short reprint work.
leads.drafted_email:
Dear Chad,

This is an introduction, nothing more. Open Letter is a literary press working in translation, and presses like yours are the work we do most. We would like to be on the list of printers you quote when your next season is ready. That is the entire ask.

I'm reaching out from Cushing-Malloy in Ann Arbor, Michigan. We've been manufacturing books for independent and university presses since 1948.

Here's what makes us different:

  • One person manages your project start to finish: same contact for quoting, production, proofing, and delivery. Nothing falls through the cracks.
  • 20 working days from approved files to finished books, delivered to your door.
  • Backlist reprints in small quantities, so your library collections stay in stock without filling a warehouse.

Not sure where to start? That's exactly why we're here. We'd love to talk through your options and provide a quote for your next book. To get started, just reply to this email or click https://cushing-malloy.com/request-quote/ to submit your details.

Warm regards,

Steve Olko
Director of Sales
solko@cushing-malloy.com
P: (810) 296-7133
Cushing-Malloy, Inc.  |  1350 N. Main, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
cushing-malloy.com   –  Where Books Are Bound For Greatness®

leads.drafted_email:
Dear Blue Cedar Press Team,

Small presses generally find a printer because another press recommended one. We would rather introduce ourselves than wait for that. Blue Cedar is an independent Kansas publisher. We would like the chance to quote your next title.

I'm reaching out from Cushing-Malloy in Ann Arbor, Michigan. We've been manufacturing books for independent and university presses since 1948.

Here's what makes us different:

  • One person manages your project start to finish: same contact for quoting, production, proofing, and delivery. Nothing falls through the cracks.
  • 20 working days from approved files to finished books, delivered to your door.
  • Runs starting at 250 copies, sized for a press selling regionally rather than nationally.

Not sure where to start? That's exactly why we're here. We'd love to talk through your options and provide a quote for your next book. To get started, just reply to this email or click https://cushing-malloy.com/request-quote/ to submit your details.

Warm regards,

Steve Olko
Director of Sales
solko@cushing-malloy.com
P: (810) 296-7133
Cushing-Malloy, Inc.  |  1350 N. Main, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
cushing-malloy.com   –  Where Books Are Bound For Greatness®

leads.subj: Printing your Asheville list -- Ann Arbor short run manufacturing
leads.opener: We do not know when your next title goes to print, so treat this as early notice. If you are quoting now, we would like to be included. If not, file us for the next one. Pisgah is an independent North Carolina publisher, and presses that size are what we manufacture for.
leads.b3: Reprints on short notice when a regional title sells through faster than planned.

Reed is the named editor and publisher. Mailbox individually confirmed with a control address rejected.
leads.pitch: Regional micro press whose local sell through creates unpredictable reprint demand.

leads.name: North Star Press of St. Cloud
leads.title: Owner and Editor
leads.subj: Printing your Minnesota list -- short run manufacturing from Ann Arbor
leads.opener: Short run publishers are finding fewer printers willing to quote them as trade minimums climb. That is the market as it has been described to us. North Star Press is an independent Minnesota publisher. We would like to show you what a run costs at your quantities.
leads.b3: Midwest delivery on a short lane, which keeps freight off your unit cost.
leads.notes: Long running Minnesota independent publishing regional fiction, memoir and Upper Midwest nonfiction at roughly 12 titles a year. Same regional freight lane as Ann Arbor, which is a real line item at these quantities. GENERIC ADDRESS FLAG: info@ is the published address; Liz Dwyer is the named owner and editor. Mailbox individually confirmed with a control address rejected.
leads.pitch: Established Midwest regional publisher at 12 titles a year on a short freight lane.
